concept: Inspire / To Breathe Generative Life Into Being
description: The act of filling someone with the urge or ability to do or feel something, especially to do something creative. It is the transmission of generative, life-affirming energy.
sound_symbolism: The skillful breath 'phe' knows exactly how to kindle dormant sparks - not forcing but coaxing flame from ember. This awakening rises through 'su', the hopeful sound of possibility taking wing, potential transforming into flight. The final 'a' releases all limits, creating boundless space for what might emerge. Three syllables trace inspiration's arc: skillful touch, rising hope, infinite opening - the way one candle lights another without diminishing its own flame.
grammatical_notes: Functions as both verb (to inspire) and noun (inspiration) without morphological change, similar to Mandarin qǐfā (启发) and Arabic ilhām (إلهام). The same form expresses both the act of inspiring and the creative spark itself.
